<!-- audit-checked: 2026-06-27 sha=e1e2218c6 -->
# SEC API

SEC API is a primary-source, agent-native SEC and factor data platform with REST, CLI, hosted MCP, compact AI-ready responses, and a 50-prompt persona library spanning law firms, investment managers, sophisticated investors, insurance, and PR firms.

## Best entry points

- Start with an agent (one-paste onramp): https://secapi.ai/agents
- Product home: https://secapi.ai/
- Developer hub: https://secapi.ai/developers
- Pricing: https://secapi.ai/pricing
- Drop-in agent rules file: https://secapi.ai/agents/AGENTS.md
- Installable agent skills: `npx skills add secapi-ai/secapi-skills`
- Agent prompt library: https://docs.secapi.ai/agents/prompt-library
- Full docs export (every page, one file): https://docs.secapi.ai/llms-full.txt
- Docs home: https://docs.secapi.ai/
- SEC EDGAR API guide: https://docs.secapi.ai/seo/sec-edgar-api
- 13F data API guide: https://docs.secapi.ai/seo/13f-data-api
- SEC filing search API evaluation: https://docs.secapi.ai/evaluate/sec-filing-search-api
- 13F API evaluation: https://docs.secapi.ai/evaluate/13f-api
- Pricing and limits evaluation: https://docs.secapi.ai/evaluate/pricing-and-limits
- Migrate from sec-api.io: https://docs.secapi.ai/migrate-from-sec-api
- API health: `/healthz`
- Factor quickstart: https://docs.secapi.ai/factor-quickstart
- Factor API reference: https://docs.secapi.ai/api-reference/factors
- Portfolio API reference: https://docs.secapi.ai/api-reference/portfolio
- FactorsToday migration: https://docs.secapi.ai/migrate-from-factorstoday
- CLI guide: https://docs.secapi.ai/cli
- MCP server: `/mcp`
- JS SDK: https://docs.secapi.ai/javascript-sdk
- Python SDK: https://docs.secapi.ai/python-sdk

## Core API workflows

- resolve entities: `GET /v1/entities/resolve`
- search filings: `GET /v1/filings`
- latest filing: `GET /v1/filings/latest`
- latest section: `GET /v1/filings/latest/sections/{sectionKey}`
- facts: `GET /v1/facts`
- statements: `GET /v1/statements/{statementKey}`
- 13F: `GET /v1/owners/13f`
- compensation: `GET /v1/compensation`
- artifacts: `POST /v1/artifacts`
- billing + plan caps: `GET /v1/billing` (rate caps, monthly budget headroom)
- delivery events: `GET /v1/delivery/events`
- event catalog: `GET /v1/event_types`

## Factor and portfolio workflows

Use these as top-level agent entry points for factor data, factor discovery, portfolio attribution, hedging, optimization, and model analysis. For factor analytic, portfolio, and model-analysis endpoints that expose response controls, prefer `response_mode=compact` by default. Add `include=trust` when the answer must preserve freshness, materialization, methodology, degraded-state, and provenance metadata; add `include=series` only when the caller needs chart/table time series.

- factor catalog: `GET /v1/factors/catalog`
- factor returns: `GET /v1/factors/returns`
- single-factor history: `GET /v1/factors/history/{factorKey}`
- multi-factor sparklines: `GET /v1/factors/sparklines`
- intraday factor snapshots: `GET /v1/factors/returns/intraday`
- factor correlations: `GET /v1/factors/correlations`
- extreme factor moves: `GET /v1/factors/extreme-moves`
- extreme factor pairs: `GET /v1/factors/extreme-pairs`
- exposures/loadings: `GET /v1/factors/exposures`, `GET /v1/stocks/{ticker}/loadings`
- related stocks: `GET /v1/factors/related-stocks`
- factor decomposition: `GET /v1/factors/decomposition`
- factor dashboard: `GET /v1/factors/dashboard`
- regime performance: `GET /v1/factors/regime-performance`
- factor pair spread: `GET /v1/factors/pairs`, `GET /v1/factors/pair-history/{f1}/{f2}`
- bulk factor returns: `GET /v1/factors/bulk-download`
- custom factor discovery: `POST /v1/factors/custom`
- portfolio analysis: `POST /v1/portfolio/analyze`
- portfolio attribution: `POST /v1/portfolio/attribution`
- portfolio hedge: `POST /v1/portfolio/hedge`
- portfolio optimization: `POST /v1/portfolio/optimize`
- portfolio stress test: `POST /v1/portfolio/stress-test`
- model factor analysis: `POST /v1/models/factor-analysis`

## MCP workflows

Selected tools live on `https://api.secapi.ai/mcp`:

- entity: `entities.resolve`
- filings: `filings.search`, `filings.latest`, `filings.render`, `filings.export`, `sections.get`, `sections.search`
- xbrl + statements: `facts.get`, `statements.get`
- company financials: `companies.financials`, `companies.ratios`, `companies.income_statements`, `companies.balance_sheets`, `companies.cash_flow_statements`
- ownership + insiders: `owners.get_13f`, `owners.compare_13f`, `insiders.list`
- compensation: `comp.list`, `comp.compare`
- artifacts: `artifacts.bundle`
- market data: `market.search`, `market.snapshots`
- macro: `macro.indicators`, `macro.high_signal_pack`, `macro.regimes`
- factors: `factors.catalog`, `factors.returns`, `factors.history`, `factors.sparklines`, `factors.correlations`, `factors.extreme_moves`, `factors.extreme_pairs`, `factors.exposures`, `factors.bulk_returns`, `factors.pair_spread`, `factors.regime_performance`, `factors.dashboard`, `factors.decomposition`, `factors.related_stocks`
- portfolio: `portfolio.analyze`, `portfolio.attribution`, `portfolio.hedge`, `portfolio.optimize`, `portfolio.stress_test`
- intelligence: `intelligence.query`, `intelligence.footnotes` (topics: lease, tax, revenue, debt_covenant, segment)
- dilution: `dilution.score.get`, `dilution.coverage.get`, `dilution.events.list`, `dilution.events.get`, `dilution.share_float_history.list`, `dilution.cash_position.list`, `dilution.convertibles.list`, `dilution.warrants.list`, `dilution.lockups.list`, `dilution.rofr.list`, `dilution.corporate_actions.list`, `dilution.reverse_splits.list`, `dilution.nasdaq_compliance.list`, `dilution.ratings.list`
- signals: `signals.dilution.get`, `signals.dilution.enhanced.get` (SEC API Dilution Score — factor-weighted 0-100 derived metric + factor-model context)
- sec events: `events.voting_results` (8-K Item 5.07 structured proposals)
- special situations: `situations.list`, `situations.search`, `situations.get`, `situations.feed`, `situations.calendar`, `situations.stats`, `situations.performance`, `situations.export`, `situations.watch`, `situations.issues`, `situations.issue`, `situations.underwrite`
- fund letters: `fund_letters.list`, `fund_letters.search`, `fund_letters.semantic`, `fund_letters.get`, `fund_letters.document`, `fund_letters.theses`, `fund_letters.managers`, `fund_letters.manager_overview`, `fund_letters.changes`

Resources include: `sec://entities/{ticker_or_cik}`, `sec://filings/latest/{ticker_or_cik}/{form}`, `sec://sections/latest/{ticker_or_cik}/{form}/{section_key}` (+ `?mode=compact|full`), `sec://statements/{ticker_or_cik}/{statement_key}/{period}`, `sec://13f/{cik}`, `sec://market/snapshot/{symbol}`, `sec://macro/{country}/{indicator_key}`, `sec://factors/returns/{factor_key}`, and `sec://intelligence/company/{ticker_or_cik}`.

## Agent prompt library

50 v1 prompts (12 investment-manager / 12 law-firm / 12 pr-firm / 8 sophisticated-investor / 6 insurance) plus 10 v2-pending entries queued for delivery alongside the dilution-tools epic + AAER MCP wrap. Discoverable at:

- Mintlify: `https://docs.secapi.ai/agents/prompt-library/{persona}`
- CLI: `secapi agents prompts list/read/copy`

## API conventions

- request correlation: `Request-Id` header
- metering classification: `SECAPI-Meter-Class` header
- auth: `x-api-key` today, WorkOS OAuth discovery later
- errors: structured JSON with `object=error`, `code`, `type`, `message`, `requestId`

## Notes for agents

- Auth with the `x-api-key` header. Never send the API key as `Authorization: Bearer` (bearer = browser/session credentials).
- Resolve entities (`GET /v1/entities/resolve`) before querying; pass the resolved CIK downstream. `companies/overview` and `companies/segments` accept only `ticker` or `cik`.
- Prefer structured endpoints and compact responses (`response_mode=compact`) over raw filing text; paginate with cursors, do not poll.
- Cite the source accession number and filing page for any filing-derived number; keep extracted facts separate from interpretation.
- Check `GET /v1/billing` and `POST /v1/billing/quote` before expensive or repeated loops; stop on a budget cap or approval requirement. Back off on `429`; tolerate `503` from beta-gated surfaces.
